clinical.chief_complaints: Right upper quadrant pain radiating to right shoulder for 3 days, fever, nausea, vomiting after fatty meals
clinical.history_of_present_illness: 45-year-old male presenting with right upper quadrant colicky pain for 3 days, radiating to the right shoulder and scapula. Pain worsens after fatty meals. Associated with two episodes of vomiting and low-grade fever. Similar but milder episodes over past 6 months. No jaundice. No dark urine or clay-coloured stools. No change in bowel habits.
clinical.planned_procedure: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy without common bile duct exploration

examination_findings.general: Conscious, oriented, cooperative. Mild distress due to pain. No icterus. No lymphadenopathy.
examination_findings.cvs: S1S2 heard, no murmurs. Heart rate 92 bpm, regular.
examination_findings.rs: Clear air entry bilaterally. No adventitious sounds.
examination_findings.abdomen: Tenderness and voluntary guarding in right hypochondrium. Murphy's sign positive. No palpable gallbladder. No hepatosplenomegaly. Bowel sounds present.

investigations.result_summary: Multiple calculi seen in gallbladder (largest 14mm). Gallbladder wall thickening to 5.5mm with pericholecystic fluid. Common bile duct not dilated (4mm). No intrahepatic biliary radicle dilatation. Normal liver parenchyma.

WBC 13,800 cells/mcL (elevated). Haemoglobin 13.2 g/dL. Total bilirubin 1.1 mg/dL (normal). Direct bilirubin 0.4 mg/dL (normal). ALP 92 U/L. AST 44 U/L. ALT 38 U/L. Serum amylase 68 U/L (normal). INR 1.0.

clinical.past_medical_history: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, diagnosed 4 years ago, on oral hypoglycaemics. Well controlled. No cardiac history.
clinical.notes: Patient fit for laparoscopic surgery. Pre-anaesthetic check-up done. Diabetes optimised pre-operatively.
